event: The Muscovy Company, the first major English joint-stock trading company, is chartered.
The East India Company (EIC) is founded in London to trade in the Indian Ocean region.
The Dutch East India Company (VOC) is established, becoming the first multinational corporation.
The Bank of England is founded to act as the English Government's banker.
Adam Smith publishes 'The Wealth of Nations', a foundational text in classical economics.
The Buttonwood Agreement is signed, laying the foundation for the New York Stock Exchange.
The Stockton and Darlington Railway opens, the world's first public railway to use steam locomotives, boosting trade.
Procter & Gamble is founded by William Procter and James Gamble in Cincinnati, Ohio.
Lehman Brothers is founded as a general merchandise store in Montgomery, Alabama.
Western Union is founded as The New York and Mississippi Valley Printing Telegraph Company.
Nokia is founded as a single paper mill operation in southwestern Finland.
John D. Rockefeller incorporates Standard Oil, which comes to dominate the oil industry.
The Panic of 1873 triggers a severe international economic depression.
The Coca-Cola Company is established after John Stith Pemberton invents the beverage.
Nintendo is founded in Kyoto, Japan, to produce hanafuda playing cards.
General Electric is formed through the merger of Edison General Electric and Thomson-Houston Electric Company.
The Dow Jones Industrial Average is first published by Charles Dow.
J.P. Morgan creates United States Steel Corporation, the world's first billion-dollar company.
The Ford Motor Company is incorporated by Henry Ford and investors.
The Pure Food and Drug Act is passed in the US, regulating business practices in the food and pharmaceutical industries.
The Panic of 1907 severely damages the US economy, leading to the creation of the Federal Reserve System.
The Supreme Court orders the dissolution of Standard Oil under the Sherman Antitrust Act.
The Federal Reserve System is established in the United States.
Thomas Watson Sr. joins the Computing-Tabulating-Recording Company, later renamed IBM.
The Walt Disney Company is founded as the Disney Brothers Cartoon Studio.
Time magazine is first published by Briton Hadden and Henry Luce.
Pan American World Airways is founded as a scheduled air mail and passenger service operating between Florida and Cuba.
The Wall Street Crash triggers the Great Depression.
Unilever is formed by the merger of Margarine Unie and Lever Brothers.
The Glass-Steagall Act is passed in the US, separating commercial and investment banking.
Toyota Motor Corporation is founded by Kiichiro Toyoda as a spinoff from his father's company to create automobiles.
Samsung is founded by Lee Byung-chul in Daegu, Japanese Korea, as a trading company.
McDonald's is founded as a restaurant in San Bernardino, California.
The Bretton Woods system establishes international financial rules and creates the IMF and World Bank.
Kaiser Permanente, one of the largest modern healthcare organizations, is opened to the public.
Sony is founded as Tokyo Tsushin Kogyo in a department store building in Tokyo.
The General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade (GATT) is signed.
Frank McNamara and Ralph Schneider introduce the Diners Club card, the world's first multipurpose charge card.
Hugh Hefner publishes the first issue of Playboy magazine.
Ray Kroc opens his first franchised McDonald's restaurant in Des Plaines, Illinois.
Bank of America launches the BankAmericard, the first successful modern credit card, later known as Visa.
Sam Walton opens the first Walmart store in Rogers, Arkansas.
Phil Knight and Bill Bowerman found Blue Ribbon Sports, which later becomes Nike.
Intel Corporation is founded by Gordon Moore and Robert Noyce.
The first ATM in the United States is installed by Chemical Bank in New York.
The NASDAQ, the world's first electronic stock market, begins trading.
The 1973 oil crisis leads to soaring fuel prices and major economic shifts worldwide.
The first commercial use of a UPC barcode scanner occurs at a Marsh supermarket in Ohio.
Bill Gates and Paul Allen found Microsoft in Albuquerque, New Mexico.
Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne establish Apple Computer, Inc.
Oracle Corporation is founded by Larry Ellison, Bob Miner, and Ed Oates.
The Sony Walkman is introduced, revolutionizing the portable consumer electronics market.
The United States initiates the deregulation of the trucking and airline industries.
MTV launches, profoundly influencing the music and television industries.
The AT&T settlement forces the breakup of the Bell System monopoly.
Apple introduces the Macintosh computer with a famous Super Bowl commercial.
Blockbuster Video opens its first store in Dallas, Texas.
'Black Monday' occurs as stock markets around the world crash.
Tim Berners-Lee invents the World Wide Web, paving the way for e-commerce.
The East German Mark is abolished and replaced by the Deutsche Mark as German reunification nears.
Jeff Bezos founds Amazon in Bellevue, Washington, initially as an online bookstore.
Pierre Omidyar launches AuctionWeb, which later becomes eBay.
Netflix is founded by Reed Hastings and Marc Randolph as a mail-based rental business.
Larry Page and Sergey Brin found Google in a garage in Menlo Park, California.
The Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act repeals parts of the Glass-Steagall Act in the US.
The Dot-com bubble bursts, causing many internet-based companies to fail.
Enron files for bankruptcy in one of the largest corporate accounting scandals in history.
SpaceX is founded by Elon Musk with the goal of reducing space transportation costs.
Tesla Motors is incorporated by Martin Eberhard and Marc Tarpenning.
Mark Zuckerberg and his college roommates launch Facebook.
YouTube is founded by Chad Hurley, Steve Chen, and Jawed Karim.
Twitter is created by Jack Dorsey, Noah Glass, Biz Stone, and Evan Williams.
Apple releases the first iPhone, disrupting the mobile phone and personal computing markets.
Lehman Brothers files for bankruptcy, marking the climax of the global financial crisis.
Bitcoin, the first decentralized cryptocurrency, is launched by Satoshi Nakamoto.
Instagram is launched by Kevin Systrom and Mike Krieger.
Occupy Wall Street protests begin in New York City, drawing attention to economic inequality.
Facebook holds its initial public offering, one of the largest in technology history.
Jeff Bezos purchases The Washington Post newspaper for $250 million.
Alibaba Group holds its IPO on the New York Stock Exchange, raising a record $25 billion.
The Paris Agreement on climate change is adopted, pushing global businesses toward sustainability.
Microsoft acquires LinkedIn for $26.2 billion.
Amazon acquires Whole Foods Market for $13.7 billion.
Apple becomes the first publicly traded US company to reach a $1 trillion market capitalization.
Saudi Aramco becomes the world's most valuable public company after its IPO.
Global stock markets crash and then rapidly recover amidst the onset of the COVID-19 pandemic.
Retail investors coordinate on Reddit to drive up the stock price of GameStop (GME).
Elon Musk acquires Twitter for $44 billion and takes the company private.
The collapse of Silicon Valley Bank becomes the second-largest bank failure in US history.
Major tech companies heavily invest and launch numerous Generative AI tools and services in a global AI boom.
